name: Statue of Liberty
desc: Neoclassical icon on Liberty Island — take the ferry for stunning harbor views

name: Central Park
desc: 843 acres of paths, lakes and meadows in the heart of Manhattan

name: Times Square
desc: The crossroads of the world — neon billboards, Broadway, and endless energy

name: Empire State Building
desc: Art Deco icon with a 86th-floor observatory and panoramic city views

name: Brooklyn Bridge
desc: Walk the iconic 1883 suspension bridge for sweeping views of Lower Manhattan

name: The Met
desc: One of the world's greatest museums — 5,000 years of art under one roof

name: One World Observatory
desc: Top-floor observatory of the Western Hemisphere's tallest building — 360° views from 1,250 ft

name: The High Line
desc: Elevated linear park on a historic freight rail line through Chelsea & Hudson Yards

name: Rockefeller Center
desc: Art Deco complex with Top of the Rock observatory and the famous skating rink

name: Grand Central Terminal
desc: Magnificent Beaux-Arts terminal with a celestial ceiling mural — NYC's busiest station

name: DUMBO
desc: Trendy waterfront district under the Manhattan Bridge with stunning skyline views

name: 9/11 Memorial & Museum
desc: Solemn twin reflecting pools mark the footprints of the Twin Towers — museum below tells the full story

name: Freehand New York
desc: Stylish 4-star hotel in the Flatiron District with a rooftop bar and vibrant common spaces

name: Wall Street
desc: The historic heart of American finance — lined with neoclassical buildings and bronze bull sculptures

name: New York Stock Exchange
desc: The world's largest stock exchange, fronted by a grand Corinthian colonnade on Broad Street

name: Chrysler Building
desc: Art Deco masterpiece with a gleaming stainless steel crown — one of NYC's most beloved skyscrapers

name: Madison Square Park
desc: Leafy urban park at the Flatiron crossroads — great for people-watching and the original Shake Shack

name: Flatiron Building
desc: Iconic triangular 1902 skyscraper at the convergence of Broadway and Fifth Avenue
